action.reasoning: Observation: Greedy heuristic gets 10% success rate on outdoor maps, explored_rate ~0.97 but can't reach 0.9999. Analysis: When all neighbors have utility=0, the robot gets stuck. Need better strategy for 'last mile' - finding remaining unexplored cells. The key insight: when utility=0 for all nodes, I should look at the global map to find UNKNOWN cells and navigate toward them. Also, I should use graph traversal to reach non-neighbor nodes. Decision: Implement improved planner with: 1) frontier detection from global map, 2) path planning through graph to reach distant high-utility nodes, 3) better handling of zero-utility situations. Submit eval immediately after.
